name: Asparagus Sauce - Great Chefs
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the asparagus and snap off the white ends. Set aside all tops and half that number of ends.
2. Plunge the tops into a large pot of salted, boiling water and cook until limp but not overcooked.
- Note: Cook slightly longer than for eating. If undercooked, they will not process well; if overcooked, they will taste earthy.
3. Drain the asparagus thoroughly while still hot.
4. Run the drained asparagus through a food processor with unsalted butter for 3-4 minutes until smooth, scraping down the sides of the processor frequently.
5. Chop the asparagus ends to remove dry hard field cuts and simmer with cream. Cook slowly for 20 minutes, then strain gently.
6. Just before serving, combine the asparagus, butter, and cream. Heat slowly to just below boiling point.
7. Adjust the seasonings (salt and pepper) to taste.
Preparation time: 3/4 Hour
